intro: Superwall and OpenX sit on opposite sides of the transparency line — Superwall sells a subscription from $10 with a free tier, while OpenX only offers custom / contact-sales pricing. Superwall is remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing for iOS/Android subscription apps. OpenX is programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform for publishers. Pick Superwall when the job is mobile, apps and you accept medium lock-in; pick OpenX when it is large publishers wanting another demand source in their header bidding wrapper and you accept low lock-in. The data we have shows Superwall at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and OpenX at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Superwall is a poor fit for subscription-only apps, and OpenX is a poor fit for mobile-only app publishers needing sdk-first tooling and app-specific demand. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Superwall is for remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing; OpenX is for programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Superwall if a subscription at $10 and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to remote-configurable paywalls and paywall A/B testing for iOS/Android subscription apps.
verdict_b: Choose OpenX if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work lines up with programmatic ad exchange and supply-side platform for publishers.

faq.a: We can't verify it cleanly: Superwall starts at $10 (subscription), while OpenX (custom) doesn't publish an entry price. Request a quote from OpenX before assuming either side is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Superwall to OpenX?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Superwall carries medium lock-in and OpenX carries low lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Superwall scores higher on developer experience in our data (5/5 vs 3/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is OpenX a good alternative to Superwall?
faq.a: Yes — OpenX is a reasonable alternative to Superwall for mobile, apps. The practical differences are subscription-vs-custom billing and medium-vs-low lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat OpenX as a credible swap.
